markdown:
#  Link a Correspondence Item on a Drawing 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To link correspondence items on your personal layer of a drawing:_
      * 'Read Only' level permissions or higher on the project's Drawings tool.  
AND
      * 'Read Only' level permissions or higher on the correspondence type.
    * _To link correspondence items and publish the markups to the drawing:_
      * 'Standard' level permissions or higher on the project's Drawings tool.  
AND
      * 'Read Only' level permissions or higher on the correspondence type.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. Click **Open** next to the drawing you want to mark up.  
_Note:_ You can also click **Info** next to the drawing and then click **Open in Viewer / Markup** in the sidebar.
  3. Click the **Markup** tab if it is not already selected.  
![Markup In The Drawing Toolbar](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/690eed7f1b889b05c32e8c1fa29218f8/97849b368c8d5070d7b9f4577cc64966/markup-in-the-drawing-toolbar.jpg?fm=webp&h=40)
  4. Click the markup tool that you want to use (such as Cloud, Arrow, or Text). See [Mark Up a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing).
  5. Add a markup to the relevant area of the drawing.
  6. Click the **paperclip** ![Icon Link Item](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b232902e86447bbdd16b569625f86143/06d7a025357ad7eef896718f46ed98ab/icon-link-item.png?fm=webp&h=16) icon and select **Correspondence**.
  7. Select a correspondence type.
  8. In the **Link a [Correspondence Type]** field, search for and select the correspondence item that you want to add to the markup. 
#####  Tip
Double-click in this field to see a list of all correspondence items that you have access to that were created with the correspondence type that you selected.
  9. Click the name of a correspondence item to link it to the drawing markup.
  10. If you want others to be able to view the linked correspondence item on the drawing and in the **Links** section on the correspondence item, select the markup and click **Publish**. See [Publish Personal Drawing Markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/publish-personal-drawing-markups).


## See Also
  * [View a Correspondence Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/correspondence-project/tutorials/view-a-correspondence-item)
  * [Add a Related Item to a Correspondence Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/correspondence-project/tutorials/add-a-related-item-to-a-correspondence-item)
  * [Create and Link an RFI, Change Event, or a Different Correspondence Item to an Existing Correspondence Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/correspondence-project/tutorials/create-an-link-an-rfi-change-event-or-a-different-correspondence-item-to-an-existing-correspondence-item)
title: Link a Correspondence Item on a Drawing
description: How to link a correspondence item to a section of a drawing.
